freeze.reason: staging split for real-project seeds; promotion to clean60 requires 60 admitted cases, final independent review, and prompt freeze

selection_protocol.case_source_policy: Stage only real-project seeds with upstream project, commit, path, and license provenance.
selection_protocol.admission_order_policy: Review and smoke candidates before any clean LLM evaluation; promotion to clean60 happens only after the full 60-case split is assembled.
selection_protocol.review_policy: Require independent review of every candidate's bug, oracle coverage, provenance, and clean-split eligibility before promotion.
selection_protocol.model_result_blinding_policy: Do not run or inspect source-only, raw, trimmed-raw, or structured repair outcomes while admitting or excluding candidates.
selection_protocol.near_duplicate_policy: Reject semantic near-duplicates of dev40, bpfix-bench replay cases, or already staged candidates even when exact file hashes differ.

candidate_seed_ledger.notes: Minimized the process-event ringbuf reserve/fill/submit pattern into a deterministic TC harness; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.
Minimized the stdiocap syscall-exit map/ringbuf/user-copy workflow into a tracepoint helper-memory contract case; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.
Minimized the map-backed policy decision and branch-merge proof shape into a deterministic TC harness; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.
Minimized the LSM bpf policy-deny and ringbuf violation event path into a source-first proof-lifecycle case; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the Cilium overlay datapath packet-revalidation pattern into a deterministic TC harness that concretizes the invalidating helper with bpf_skb_change_proto; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the xdp-filter macro and inline parser shape into a deterministic XDP harness where the macro proves bounds for a fixed L4 pointer but the rejected read uses an IHL-derived pointer; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.
Minimized the opensnoop tracepoint stack-capture workflow into a single XDP-section boundary failure where bpf_get_stack is rejected by program type; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the dynptr TC parser and ring-buffer dynptr workflow into a reference-lifecycle case requiring discard or submit on every ringbuf dynptr path; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the sched_switch CPU-contention observer into a tracepoint proof-lifecycle case where a nullable per-CPU hash slot proof is lost across a branch merge; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the user-ringbuf drain callback and added a small payload-copy step to create a dynptr payload proof-lifecycle case where bpf_dynptr_data returns mem_or_null and the proof is branch-local; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the browsertrace syscall-exit map lookup, timestamp delta, user-copy, ringbuf, and cleanup workflow into a branch-merge map-value proof-lifecycle case; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.
Minimized the sslsniff uretprobe saved-buffer, timestamp, scratch-map, user-copy, cleanup, and perf-output workflow into a helper-memory contract case; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized Cilium's IPv6 extension-header inline parsing into an XDP source/object-correlation case where a fixed L4 bounds check does not prove the extension-derived variable L4 UDP read; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the xdpdump XDP packet metadata capture path into a helper/config boundary case where bpf_perf_event_output receives a non-perf-event-array map; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the ActPlane taint-engine bpf_loop tokenizer shape into an XDP loop-callback bound case where the callback receives an unbounded loop index and must prove the stack scratch-slot bound locally; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the xdp-tools xdp_load_bytes helper probe into an XDP helper-memory contract case where bpf_xdp_load_bytes is asked to write beyond the stack scratch buffer; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

Minimized the pidhide tail-call dispatch and prog-array workflow into a runnable XDP multi-program harness where bpf_tail_call receives a normal array map; no LLM repair outcomes were used for admission.

seed_exclusion_ledger.notes: Rejected before admission because the file lives in a nested kernel submodule and the default provenance gate cannot verify it from the namei_ext top-level pinned ref/path; no LLM repair outcomes were inspected.

Rejected before admission because the candidate file exists in a nested or dirty worktree but not at that path in the top-level pinned ref, so the default provenance gate cannot verify the source; no LLM repair outcomes were inspected.

Rejected before admission for this environment-boundary shape because the attempted XDP bpf_probe_read_user variant verifier-loads on the pinned kernel/toolchain; no LLM repair outcomes were inspected.
Rejected before admission because the upstream source file lacks an SPDX-License-Identifier header required by the real-project provenance gate; no LLM repair outcomes were inspected.

cases.origin: real-project seed from agentsight process tracer ring-buffer event emission

review.bug_summary: The program writes a reserved process_event before proving bpf_ringbuf_reserve returned non-null.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, verifier load, TC functional returns, bpf_ringbuf_reserve/submit presence, and a successful-verifier proof predicate that the same 16-byte ringbuf record has mark=7 written at offset 12 before submit.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from agentsight process.bpf.c ringbuf event emission and minimized into a deterministic TC harness; README and manifest distinguish this from an original TC bug.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in deterministic TC harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved ringbuf reserve/fill/submit process-event shape while replacing tracepoint context with a deterministic TC packet harness.

functional_semantics: IPv4 packet returns TC_ACT_SHOT
non-IPv4 packet returns TC_ACT_OK
repair must keep process-event ringbuf submit path
verifier_reject_reason: R0 is ringbuf_mem_or_null after bpf_ringbuf_reserve
buggy program writes to the ringbuf record before a null check
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads
bpftool prog run returns the expected TC actions
successful verifier log shows a 16-byte ringbuf_mem record with mark=7 stored at offset 12 before bpf_ringbuf_submit

cases.origin: real-project seed from agentsight stdiocap syscall-exit user-buffer capture

review.bug_summary: The syscall-exit tracepoint copies 16 bytes into an 8-byte ring-buffer payload at offset 16, so the verifier rejects bpf_probe_read_user with mem_size=24 off=16 size=16.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom oracle requires compile, tracepoint verifier load, source-level preservation of the io_args lookup, ringbuf reserve, bpf_probe_read_user, submit, and cleanup delete, plus successful-verifier predicates proving the probe destination range fits the reserved event and that metadata plus user copy are submitted.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from agentsight stdiocap.bpf.c, which stores syscall arguments in a map, reserves a ringbuf event on exit, copies user data into the event, submits it, and deletes pending state. The minimized harness preserves that workflow while isolating the helper length contract.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in tracepoint harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved stdiocap's syscall-exit map lookup, ringbuf event emission, user-memory copy into event payload, submit, and cleanup delete while reducing the full tracer to a single helper-memory-contract failure.

functional_semantics: repair must load as a syscall-exit tracepoint, not as XDP or TC
repair must preserve io_args lookup and cleanup delete
repair must preserve ringbuf event emission and the bpf_probe_read_user copy into the event payload
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_probe_read_user writes size bytes into the destination pointer supplied in R1
buggy program passes size 16 for event->buf even though the ringbuf event has only 8 bytes available at offset 16
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as a tracepoint program
successful verifier log shows io_args lookup before ringbuf reserve before bpf_probe_read_user before ringbuf submit before io_args delete
successful verifier log proves the bpf_probe_read_user destination range fits inside the reserved ringbuf event

cases.origin: real-project seed from ActPlane map-backed policy state and branch-dependent decision

review.bug_summary: The map lookup is proved non-null only on the IPv4 branch, then the same map-value pointer is read after a branch merge.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, verifier load, four TC functional tests with different configured drop_proto values, and successful-verifier predicates proving the repair keeps map-value read and state update behavior.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from ActPlane process.bpf.c policy/map state patterns and minimized into a deterministic TC harness; README and manifest distinguish this from an original TC bug.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in deterministic TC harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved map-backed policy lookup, state update, and branch-dependent decision shape while replacing LSM/tracepoint context with a deterministic TC packet harness.

functional_semantics: packet action depends on the configured policy_map drop_proto value
IPv4 path updates seen_ipv4 state
non-matching protocol returns TC_ACT_OK
verifier_reject_reason: R0 is map_value_or_null after bpf_map_lookup_elem
buggy program reads policy->drop_proto after a branch merge where the non-null proof is not available on every path
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads
bpftool prog run returns expected TC actions for IPv4 and ARP under two map configurations
successful verifier log shows drop_proto read from map value and seen_ipv4 state update

cases.origin: real-project seed from ActPlane LSM bpf policy enforcement and ring-buffer violation emission

review.bug_summary: The LSM policy path reaches ring-buffer event field writes with the bpf_ringbuf_reserve result still typed as ringbuf_mem_or_null on one branch.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom oracle requires compile, LSM verifier load, source-level preservation of the LSM hook, protected-pid map lookup, ringbuf reserve/submit, and deny return, plus successful-verifier predicates proving lookup-reserve-submit order, event field writes before submit, and -EPERM reaching exit.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from ActPlane process.bpf.c LSM bpf enforcement and ring-buffer violation emission. The minimized harness preserves the protected-pid map check, LSM deny policy, and violation event workflow without requiring behavior absent from the upstream seed.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in LSM harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved ActPlane's LSM bpf enforcement hook, protected-pid map lookup, ring-buffer violation event emission, and deny return while reducing the full taint engine to a single proof-lifecycle failure.

functional_semantics: repair must keep the LSM bpf hook, not convert the program to XDP or TC
repair must keep the protected-pid map lookup before policy enforcement
repair must keep ringbuf violation event submission and the -EPERM deny return
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_ringbuf_reserve returns ringbuf_mem_or_null
buggy program checks !event only under an additional privileged condition, so the unprivileged branch reaches event writes without a verifier-visible non-null proof
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as an LSM program
successful verifier log shows call bpf_map_lookup_elem#1 before call bpf_ringbuf_reserve#131 before call bpf_ringbuf_submit#132
successful verifier log shows event fields written before submit and the -EPERM deny value reaching exit

cases.origin: real-project seed from Cilium overlay datapath packet revalidation after packet rewrites

review.bug_summary: The program calls bpf_skb_change_proto and then writes through a stale pre-helper skb packet pointer.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, verifier load, three TC functional returns, bpf_skb_change_proto presence, and a successful-verifier proof predicate that the IPv4 ethertype is stored through a reloaded packet pointer after the helper.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from Cilium's overlay datapath pattern of revalidating packet data after packet rewrites; the harness concretizes that production provenance rule with bpf_skb_change_proto in a deterministic TC program.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in deterministic TC harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved Cilium's production pattern of reacquiring and revalidating skb packet data/data_end after datapath rewrites while replacing the full overlay pipeline with a small bpf_skb_change_proto TC harness.

functional_semantics: IPv6 UDP packet returns TC_ACT_SHOT after the protocol rewrite path
IPv6 TCP packet returns TC_ACT_OK
IPv4 packet returns TC_ACT_OK
repair must keep the post-helper Ethernet protocol rewrite
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_skb_change_proto invalidates existing skb packet pointers
buggy program writes eth->h_proto through the stale pre-helper packet pointer
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads
bpftool prog run returns expected TC actions for IPv6 UDP, IPv6 TCP, and IPv4 packets
successful verifier log shows bpf_skb_change_proto and a later ethertype store through a reloaded packet pointer

cases.origin: real-project seed from xdp-tools xdp-filter macro and inline parser code

review.bug_summary: The macro proves room for a fixed UDP header pointer, but the rejected inline UDP destination read uses a different pointer derived from the IPv4 IHL field.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, verifier load, seven XDP functional packet-run cases including IPv4 options/non-options and DNS/non-DNS UDP, and a successful-verifier proof predicate that the repair loads UDP dest from a variable IHL-derived packet pointer at offset +2.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from xdp-tools xdpfilt_prog.h, which uses macro-wrapped checks and inline parser/verdict helpers; the minimized harness preserves that source/object correlation shape without claiming this exact bug was present upstream.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in deterministic XDP harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved xdp-filter's macro-wrapped bounds checks, inline UDP verdict helper, and parser pointer flow while replacing the full generated filter program with a small deterministic XDP packet harness.

functional_semantics: IPv4 UDP packets to destination port 53 return XDP_DROP
IPv4 UDP packets to destination port 123 return XDP_PASS
IPv4 packets with IHL options preserve the same UDP destination semantics
truncated UDP, TCP, and non-IPv4 packets return XDP_PASS
verifier_reject_reason: XDPFILT_REQUIRE_ROOM proves bounds for checked_l4 derived from iph + 1
actual_l4 is derived from the IPv4 IHL field and has no verifier-visible packet range proof at the UDP destination read
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads
bpftool prog run returns expected XDP actions for seven packet fixtures
successful verifier log shows a u16 UDP destination load at offset +2 from a variable IHL-derived packet pointer

cases.origin: real-project seed from bpftime opensnoop tracepoint stack capture and perf-event output

review.bug_summary: The minimized opensnoop workflow calls bpf_get_stack with BPF_F_USER_STACK from an XDP section, where the verifier rejects the helper by program type.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom oracle requires compile, verifier reject for the buggy XDP helper capability error, and a successful repair that loads as a syscall-exit tracepoint while the verifier log shows start-map lookup, bpf_get_stack#67 with r4=256, and bpf_perf_event_output#25 in lookup-stack-output order.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from bpftime opensnoop.bpf.c, which stores open arguments, captures a user stack, and emits an event; the minimized harness preserves that tracing workflow while moving it into a deterministic program-type boundary failure.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in environment-boundary harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved opensnoop's start-map lookup, user-stack capture through bpf_get_stack, and perf-event output while replacing the full multi-section tracer with a single XDP-section program-type boundary failure.

functional_semantics: repair must load as a syscall-exit tracepoint, not as XDP
repair must preserve opensnoop-style start-map lookup before stack capture
repair must preserve user-stack capture and perf-event output
verifier_reject_reason: buggy program places a tracing stack-capture workflow in an XDP section
current verifier rejects bpf_get_stack#67 with program of this type cannot use helper
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads
successful verifier log shows call bpf_map_lookup_elem#1 before call bpf_get_stack#67 before call bpf_perf_event_output#25
successful verifier log shows bpf_get_stack receives BPF_F_USER_STACK via r4=256

cases.origin: real-project seed from bpf-developer-tutorial dynptr TC parser and ring-buffer dynptr event emission

review.bug_summary: The program reserves a ring-buffer dynptr and can return from reserve and header-write error paths without submitting or discarding the dynptr, so the verifier rejects the exit with an unreleased reference.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, TC verifier load, four packet-run outcomes under map configurations, dynptr skb parse/read helper presence, ringbuf dynptr reserve/write/submit presence, and proof predicates that reserve and write error paths reach bpf_ringbuf_discard_dynptr.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from bpf-developer-tutorial dynptr_tc.bpf.c, which demonstrates TC parsing with skb dynptrs, payload snapshot reads, and ring-buffer dynptr records. The minimized harness preserves that workflow while isolating the reference lifecycle contract.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in deterministic TC dynptr harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved TC dynptr packet parsing, config-map policy, payload snapshot read, and ring-buffer dynptr reserve/write/submit workflow while reducing the tutorial example to a reference-lifecycle verifier failure.

functional_semantics: configured TCP destination port returns TC_ACT_SHOT
non-matching TCP destination port returns TC_ACT_OK
non-IPv4 packets return TC_ACT_OK
ring-buffer disabled config preserves the drop decision without exercising the event path
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_ringbuf_reserve_dynptr creates a dynptr reference that must be submitted or discarded even on reserve failure
buggy program returns from reserve and header-write error paths with the dynptr reference still live
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads
bpftool prog run returns expected TC actions for blocked TCP, unblocked TCP, ringbuf-disabled blocked TCP, and non-IPv4 packets
successful verifier log shows bpf_dynptr_from_skb, bpf_dynptr_slice, bpf_dynptr_read, bpf_ringbuf_reserve_dynptr, bpf_dynptr_write, bpf_ringbuf_discard_dynptr, and bpf_ringbuf_submit_dynptr
successful verifier log proves reserve-error and write-error paths reach bpf_ringbuf_discard_dynptr

cases.origin: real-project seed from nccl-eBPF sched_switch CPU contention observer

review.bug_summary: The sched_switch observer proves the per-CPU hash slot non-null only inside the prev_pid < 0 branch; after the merge, the elapsed-window read can still see slot as map_value_or_null.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom oracle requires compile, tracepoint verifier load, source-level preservation of the config filter, target-keyed per-CPU slot lookup, timestamp/CPU helpers, mmapable state_map publish, and successful-verifier predicates proving slot field updates after a non-null proof and stack-built state_map update.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from nccl-eBPF's GPL sched_switch CPU contention observer. The minimized harness preserves the config-map filter, per-CPU slot accounting, contention classification, and mmapable state publication while using a per-target per-CPU hash slot to make the lookup nullable and exercise the intended proof-lifecycle failure.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in tracepoint observer harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved nccl-eBPF's sched_switch target filtering, rolling CPU-window accounting, contention classification, and mmapable state publication while reducing the observer to a single branch-merge nullable-slot failure.

functional_semantics: repair must load as a sched_switch tracepoint, not as XDP or TC
repair must preserve config_map target filtering and target-keyed per-CPU slot lookup
repair must preserve rolling-window slot accounting and mmapable state_map publication
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_map_lookup_elem on the per-CPU hash slot returns map_value_or_null
buggy program checks slot only inside one prev_pid branch and then dereferences slot after the branch merge
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as a tracepoint program
successful verifier log shows map lookup, ktime, CPU id, and state_map update helpers in observer order
successful verifier log proves slot field updates happen after a verifier-visible non-null proof
successful verifier log shows state_map updated from a stack-built contention state

cases.origin: real-project seed from bpf-developer-tutorial user-ringbuf dynptr callback

review.bug_summary: The user-ringbuf dynptr callback obtains a 24-byte payload pointer with bpf_dynptr_data; the non-null proof is established only on the current_pid == 0 branch, then msg->op and msg->comm are read after the branch merge where the verifier still tracks the pointer as mem_or_null.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom oracle requires compile and tracepoint verifier load, preservation of user-ringbuf drain, dynptr payload consumption via bpf_dynptr_data or checked bpf_dynptr_read, kernel-ringbuf reserve/submit, PID attribution, exact op/comm payload-offset to record-offset flow, and submit of the reserved ringbuf reference. The dynptr_read alternative requires a verifier-visible successful return check before reserve/copy.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from bpf-developer-tutorial's GPL user-ringbuf example at the pinned commit/path. The minimized harness preserves the upstream user-ringbuf drain callback and kernel-ringbuf event workflow, and explicitly adds a small payload-copy step so the heldout case exercises dynptr payload proof lifecycle rather than only helper availability.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in user-ringbuf dynptr callback harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the user-ringbuf drain, dynptr callback, kernel-ringbuf reserve/submit, and PID attribution while adding a small user-payload copy so the minimized callback becomes a branch-merge nullable dynptr payload failure.

functional_semantics: repair must load as a sys_exit_kill tracepoint program
repair must preserve user_ringbuf drain into a dynptr callback
repair must preserve kernel_ringbuf reserve/submit, PID attribution, and the injected op/comm payload copy
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_dynptr_data returns mem_or_null for the user-ringbuf payload
buggy callback checks the payload pointer only on one current_pid branch and reads msg fields after the branch merge
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as a tracepoint program
successful verifier log shows bpf_user_ringbuf_drain, bpf_dynptr_data or bpf_dynptr_read, bpf_ringbuf_reserve, and bpf_ringbuf_submit
successful verifier log proves op/comm payload values flow into the kernel ringbuf record after a verifier-visible non-null proof or dynptr_read copy
successful verifier log shows the kernel ringbuf record submitted from the reserved ringbuf reference

cases.origin: real-project seed from datrail-agent-monitor browsertrace syscall-exit workflow

review.bug_summary: The browsertrace-style syscall-exit program looks up start_ns and proves it non-null only on the pid == 0 branch, then computes event->delta_ns = ts - *tsp after the branch merge. For normal user PIDs the verifier still tracks the timestamp pointer as map_value_or_null, so the final reject is the elapsed-time dereference rather than the earlier proof-loss branch.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom oracle requires compile and tracepoint verifier load, preservation of the two map lookups, timestamp delta, ringbuf reserve/submit, bounded bpf_probe_read_user, and cleanup of both maps. Its proof predicates track verifier registers per path: the start_ns map-value load must flow into the ringbuf delta field, and the saved bufs map-value pointer must be the current R3 at bpf_probe_read_user while R1 is the ringbuf payload destination and R2 is the bounded copy length. Negative controls for constant delta, removed user copy, register clobber, and cross-path taint fail.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from DatRail's browsertrace BPF program at the pinned commit/path. The minimized harness preserves the upstream syscall-exit buffer lookup, start timestamp lookup, elapsed-time event field, bounded user copy, ringbuf event, and cleanup workflow while removing browser/NSS filters so the heldout case isolates branch-merge loss of the start_ns map-value proof.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in browsertrace syscall-exit harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the browsertrace exit workflow shape: saved user buffer lookup, start timestamp lookup, elapsed-time event field, bounded bpf_probe_read_user copy, ringbuf submit, and cleanup of both maps. The minimized harness removes browser-specific filters and NSS bookkeeping so the heldout case isolates branch-merge loss of the start_ns map-value proof.

functional_semantics: repair must load as a sys_exit_read tracepoint program
repair must preserve saved-buffer map lookup, start timestamp lookup, timestamp delta, bounded user-memory copy, ringbuf submit, and cleanup of both maps
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_map_lookup_elem returns map_value_or_null for the start_ns timestamp pointer
buggy program proves the start_ns pointer non-null only on the pid == 0 branch and reads *tsp after the branch merge
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as a tracepoint program
successful verifier log shows two map lookups, ringbuf reserve, bpf_probe_read_user, two map deletes, and ringbuf submit in browsertrace exit order
successful verifier log proves the start_ns map-value read happens after a verifier-visible non-null proof and is stored into the event delta field
successful verifier log proves the user-copy source comes from the saved bufs map value and remains bounded by MAX_BUF_SIZE

cases.origin: real-project seed from bpftime sslsniff uretprobe perf-event workflow

review.bug_summary: The sslsniff-style SSL_read uretprobe keeps a per-CPU ssl_data scratch event whose buggy layout has an 8-byte buf field at offset 56 while copy_size is bounded by MAX_BUF_SIZE=16. The verifier reaches bpf_probe_read_user with R1 at ssl_data+56 and R2 possibly 16, so the helper write can exceed the 64-byte map value.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom oracle requires compile and kprobe verifier load, preservation of saved-buffer lookup, start timestamp lookup, ssl_data scratch lookup, timestamp delta, bounded bpf_probe_read_user into data->buf, cleanup of both saved maps, and bpf_perf_event_output. Its proof predicates track verifier registers per path so the probe-read destination must be ssl_data.buf at offset 56 with offset+copy length within value_size, R3 must come from the saved bufs map value, and perf output must submit the ssl_data event after the copy path. Negative controls for removed copy, removed perf output, wrong output data, and wrong copy destination fail.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from bpftime's sslsniff uretprobe program at the pinned commit/path. The minimized harness preserves the upstream saved buffer/start timestamp maps, per-CPU scratch event, metadata fill, user copy, cleanup, and perf-event output while shrinking the scratch buffer so the heldout case isolates helper-memory range proof.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in uretprobe perf-event harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the sslsniff return-probe workflow: saved user buffer lookup, start timestamp lookup, per-CPU ssl_data scratch lookup, metadata fill, bounded bpf_probe_read_user copy, cleanup of saved maps, and bpf_perf_event_output. The minimized harness shrinks the scratch buffer so the helper-memory range proof fails while keeping the original perf-output shape.

functional_semantics: repair must load as an SSL_read uretprobe/kprobe program
repair must preserve saved-buffer lookup, start timestamp lookup, ssl_data scratch lookup, timestamp delta, bounded user-memory copy, cleanup of both saved maps, and perf-event output
verifier_reject_reason: the ssl_data per-CPU map value is 64 bytes, with buf starting at offset 56
buggy program lets bpf_probe_read_user copy up to 16 bytes into an 8-byte buf field
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as a kprobe/uretprobe program
successful verifier log shows three map lookups, bpf_probe_read_user, two map deletes, and bpf_perf_event_output in sslsniff exit order
successful verifier log proves the probe-read destination is ssl_data.buf and the range fits within the ssl_data map value
successful verifier log proves perf output submits the ssl_data event after the user-copy path

cases.origin: real-project seed from Cilium IPv6 extension-header inline parser

review.bug_summary: The Cilium-style IPv6 parser advances l4 by a packet-derived hop-by-hop option length, but the buggy program checks only the fixed no-extension L4 pointer before reading udp->dest through the extension-derived pointer. The verifier rejects the final UDP destination load because R3 carries a variable packet offset whose range is not proven.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, XDP verifier load, bpftool prog run over IPv6 UDP packets with and without hop-by-hop options, truncated extension, TCP, and IPv4 packets, plus a success-log predicate proving UDP dest is loaded from an extension-derived variable-offset packet pointer. Negative controls for removing extension parsing or bypassing the UDP policy fail, so the oracle rejects repairs that merely load while losing the production parsing semantics.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from Cilium's IPv6 extension-header parser at the pinned commit/path. The minimized harness preserves the upstream next-header classification, hop-by-hop option length calculation, inline L4 offset update, and L4 protocol handling while isolating the source/object-correlation bug in a runnable XDP port-policy case.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in Cilium IPv6 extension-header parsing harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the upstream IPv6 extension-header parsing shape: next-header classification, hop-by-hop option header length calculation, inline L4 offset update, and final L4 protocol handling. The minimized harness turns the parsed L4 offset into an XDP UDP port policy and intentionally checks the fixed no-extension L4 pointer instead of the extension-derived pointer.

functional_semantics: repair must load as an XDP program
repair must preserve IPv6 hop-by-hop extension parsing and apply the UDP destination-port policy at the final extension-derived L4 offset
verifier_reject_reason: Cilium-style inline extension parsing updates l4 by a packet-derived hop-by-hop option length
buggy program bounds-checks the fixed no-extension L4 pointer but reads udp->dest through the extension-derived variable pointer
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as an XDP program
bpftool prog run drops UDP/53 packets with and without a hop-by-hop extension and passes non-DNS, non-UDP, truncated, and IPv4 packets
successful verifier log proves the UDP destination is loaded from a packet pointer with extension-derived variable offset

cases.origin: real-project seed from xdp-tools xdpdump XDP perf-event output path

review.bug_summary: The xdpdump-style XDP metadata path computes packet metadata and cap_len flags, then calls bpf_perf_event_output with xdpdump_perf_map. The buggy map is declared BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY, so the verifier rejects the helper call because argument 2 has map_type 2 instead of the perf-event-array contract required by bpf_perf_event_output.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, XDP verifier load, bpftool prog run on short, snaplen-sized, and header-only packets returning XDP_PASS, plus load-log checks that the program still reaches bpf_perf_event_output with the xdpdump perf map and a proof predicate showing stack metadata writes and cap_len shifted into the perf-output flags. Negative controls that remove perf output or drop cap_len flag encoding fail, so the oracle rejects repairs that only make the verifier load while losing xdpdump metadata output semantics.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from xdp-tools xdpdump_xdp.c at the pinned commit and path. The minimized harness preserves the upstream XDP packet metadata capture shape, cap_len clamp, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU perf-output flags, and bpf_perf_event_output workflow while isolating the map-type contract failure in a runnable XDP program.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in xdpdump XDP perf-output harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the upstream xdpdump XDP packet metadata capture shape: packet length calculation, cap_len clamping, stack metadata record, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU perf-output flags, and bpf_perf_event_output. The minimized harness intentionally declares the perf-output map as a plain array to isolate the helper map-type contract failure.

functional_semantics: repair must load as an XDP program
repair must preserve the xdpdump metadata capture path and return XDP_PASS for representative packet sizes
verifier_reject_reason: buggy xdpdump_perf_map is declared as BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY
bpf_perf_event_output requires argument 2 to be a BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY map
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as an XDP program
bpftool prog run returns XDP_PASS for short, snaplen-sized, and header-only packets
successful verifier log reaches bpf_perf_event_output with the xdpdump perf map
successful verifier log shows stack metadata writes and cap_len shifted into the perf-output flags

cases.origin: real-project seed from ActPlane taint-engine bpf_loop tokenizer

review.bug_summary: The ActPlane-style taint tokenizer uses bpf_loop to verify a callback once, but the callback reads ctx->slots[idx] from a fixed stack scratch array while idx is an unbounded callback argument. The verifier rejects the variable-offset stack read because the callback does not establish idx < SLOT_COUNT before the read.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires verifier load, bpf_loop to remain in the accepted program, a callback-local idx <= 7 branch proof before the stack read, callback hit-counter update, and functional packet outcomes that drop an in-slot marker while passing no-marker and out-of-slot-marker packets. Negative controls that remove bpf_loop fail the oracle, and reducing the loop trip count alone still rejects, so the case tests the callback-local proof rather than a terminal-message regex.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from ActPlane taint_engine.bpf.h at the pinned commit and path. The minimized harness preserves the upstream bpf_loop tokenizer structure, callback-local scratch access, fixed slot array, marker-style scan, and result-dependent action while isolating the callback index-bound verifier failure in a runnable XDP program.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in ActPlane bpf_loop tokenizer harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the upstream taint-engine tokenizer shape: bpf_loop callback verification, callback-local scratch access, fixed slot array, marker-style scan, and result-dependent action. The minimized harness intentionally scans more loop iterations than the fixed stack scratch array can prove without a callback-local index bound.

functional_semantics: repair must load as an XDP program
repair must preserve callback-driven bpf_loop scanning over the first eight scratch slots
repair must drop packets whose first eight bytes contain marker 0xaa and pass packets without a marker or with a marker outside the scanned slots
verifier_reject_reason: bpf_loop verifies the callback with idx as an unbounded scalar
buggy callback directly reads ctx->slots[idx] from a fixed stack scratch array without a callback-local bound proof
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as an XDP program
bpftool prog run distinguishes in-slot marker, no-marker, and out-of-slot marker packets
successful verifier log reaches bpf_loop and shows a callback-local idx <= 7 proof before the variable-offset stack read
successful verifier log shows the callback updates the hit counter used by the final XDP action

cases.origin: real-project seed from xdp-tools xdp_load_bytes helper probe

review.bug_summary: The xdp-tools-style XDP helper probe allocates a ten-byte stack scratch buffer, then calls bpf_xdp_load_bytes with a fourteen-byte length. The verifier rejects the helper write with invalid write to stack R3 off=-10 size=14, so the failure is a helper memory-contract reject rather than a compile or environment artifact.
review.oracle_rationale: The oracle requires compile, XDP verifier load, three bpftool prog run marker outcomes, bpf_xdp_load_bytes presence, R4=10 at the helper call, and post-helper marker reads from r10 -1 and r10 -10. Negative controls that remove the helper or change marker semantics fail, so the oracle rejects repairs that only make the verifier load while bypassing the helper-filled stack buffer semantics.
review.provenance_rationale: Seed was derived from xdp-tools lib/util/xdp_load_bytes.bpf.c at the pinned commit and path. The minimized harness preserves the upstream XDP helper-load shape, stack scratch destination, marker-based branch on copied packet bytes, and XDP return behavior while isolating the helper length versus stack buffer failure in a runnable XDP program.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in xdp_load_bytes XDP helper harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the upstream XDP helper probe shape: use bpf_xdp_load_bytes to copy packet bytes into a stack scratch buffer, branch on the copied marker bytes, and return an XDP action from the copied contents. The minimized harness intentionally passes a helper length larger than the stack destination buffer to isolate the helper memory contract failure.

functional_semantics: repair must load as an XDP program
repair must preserve the helper-based xdp_load_bytes path and classify packets using the first and tenth copied bytes
verifier_reject_reason: buggy program allocates a ten-byte stack scratch buffer
bpf_xdp_load_bytes is asked to write fourteen bytes through the stack destination pointer
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads as an XDP program
bpftool prog run drops packets whose first byte is B and tenth byte is X while passing packets with either marker changed
successful verifier log reaches bpf_xdp_load_bytes
successful verifier log shows the helper length register R4 equals the ten-byte stack buffer size
successful verifier log shows the marker checks read from the helper-filled stack buffer at r10 -1 and r10 -10

cases.origin: real-project seed from bpf-developer-tutorial pidhide tail-call dispatch

review.bug_summary: buggy.bpf.c declares map_prog_array as BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY and then passes it to bpf_tail_call(ctx, &map_prog_array, PROG_01); the saved verifier log shows map type 2, call bpf_tail_call#12, slot register r3 = 1, and terminal kernel subsystem misconfigured verifier.
review.oracle_rationale: The custom test loads all XDP programs with pinmaps, fetches the tail_target_drop program id, updates prog-array slot 1, and runs marker and non-marker packets. The success predicate requires prog-array type 3, the bpf_tail_call helper before the target program load, r3 = 1, and the target program load, so direct-return and wrong-slot repairs are rejected.
review.provenance_rationale: The seed is auditable at bpf-developer-tutorial commit 3a722c03d5129ac212935bf5ecce118e64efcdd8, path src/24-hide/pidhide.bpf.c, SPDX BSD-3-Clause, upstream file sha256 e63f11222df61958fe300558b8957fe09eef9f4a422f80eb3e49c126b831840e. The local dirty tutorial deletions are unrelated to this source file.

provenance.seed_type: minimized real-project seed in pidhide XDP tail-call dispatch harness
provenance.minimization: Preserved the upstream pidhide multi-program tail-call shape: a prog-array map, an entry program that conditionally dispatches by bpf_tail_call, and a second target program reached through the prog array. The minimized harness uses XDP packets as deterministic inputs so bpftool prog run can exercise both fallback and tail-call paths.

functional_semantics: repair must load all programs in the object as XDP programs
repair must preserve prog-array tail-call dispatch from the entry program to the drop target
repair must drop marker packets through the populated tail-call slot and pass non-marker packets through the fallback path
verifier_reject_reason: buggy map_prog_array is declared as BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY
bpf_tail_call requires argument 2 to be a BPF_MAP_TYPE_PROG_ARRAY map
success_criteria: candidate compiles and verifier-loads with bpftool prog loadall
successful verifier log shows map_prog_array has type BPF_MAP_TYPE_PROG_ARRAY
successful verifier log reaches bpf_tail_call with slot register R3 equal to 1
successful verifier log loads and pins the tail_target_drop program
bpftool prog run returns XDP_DROP for marker packets after map slot setup and XDP_PASS for non-marker packets
